mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 300, further increases in the federal funds rate amplify real borrowing costs and trigger aggressive portfolio de-risking, causing sharp negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of cases when FEDFUNDS rises and CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which supports higher equity valuations and generates positive forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or GDPC1 growth consistently predicts negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth falling below 100,000 for two consecutive months signals labor market cooling and reduced wage pressures, prompting equity markets to price in lower earnings and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances despite the payroll slowdown signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while real sales of final goods fall indicates inventory accumulation and weakening demand, leading to immediate negative equity returns as firms face margin compression.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns dominate when INDPRO rises and RSAFS falls across multiple quarters.
